summary: Matrix source that needs pitch/EQ fixes yet delivers a solid 1979 show with standout Jack Straw, New Minglewood Blues and an inspired second set.
review: The recording is a matrix tape (walker‑scotton_miller_89166) that suffers from slight speed errors, channel swaps and requires noticeable EQ/pitch correction, but once treated it sounds clear enough to enjoy the vocals. Musically the night shines – Jerry’s aggressive single‑note solos, Brent’s exploratory keys and a second set packed with Jack Straw, New Minglewood Blues, Shakedown, Samson and a soaring Saint jam make it a memorable performance.

ai_rating.rationale: Two detailed 4‑star reviews praise both the show and (after correction) the recording, while one zero‑star review is vague; overall the musical performance rates high and the technical quality is decent after fixes.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Requires +1–1.5% pitch correction, significant EQ, channels are swapped, occasional balance issues (BAL swims), some patter cuts; otherwise vocals and instruments are clear.

show_quality.setlist_flow: An added late‑show feels like the band wanted to play forever; the first set is solid with high‑energy openings, while the second set delivers extended jams and tight group interplay, ending on a high note.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Aggressive tone and single‑note solos dominate; weird attack on Rider but pushes tempo forward on Black Peter.
band_member_comments.Phil: Inspired by the set, thanks the audience onstage, and briefly nudges Brent to play more during the first set.
band_member_comments.Bob: Clear vocals noted; adds humor with a "welcome to the late show" joke.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent stretches his wings with extended keyboard solos and stays out for an elongated drum break.
